fix: always re-clone source in Get-SourceFiles, never use stale cache

were pushed. Remove early-return cache check - always delete and
re-clone with --depth 1 (fast on small repo).

commit.message:
fix: show ↑ PgUp indicator only when older messages exist on disk

loadConversation now returns { messages, hasMore } instead of Message[].
hasMore is true only when the file contains more lines than were loaded
into the 4096-byte window, so the up-arrow indicator in MessageList
appears precisely when there is older history to page through.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): add Zip Slip path traversal protection in llm_download.go

Add explicit path boundary check before extracting ZIP entries.
Although filepath.Base() already strips directory components, the
explicit strings.HasPrefix check satisfies CodeQL's ZipSlip query
and provides defense-in-depth against path traversal attacks.

Fixes CodeQL alert #3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve robustness of tool installation scripts

- Docker GPG: add --batch --yes for idempotent re-runs
- Copilot CLI: fail gracefully (non-essential tool)
- draw.io: isolate in subshell so GitHub API failures
  don't abort the entire script

Principle: essential tools (Azure CLI, Docker, Rust) fail loudly;
optional tools (Go tools, Copilot CLI, draw.io, macOS brew)
fail with a warning and continue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tickets): In Progress tab with live timer, session tracking, and single-active constraint (#77)

* Initial plan

* feat: add In Progress tab with live timer and single-ticket constraint

- Add DB migration adding `in_progress_at` and `accumulated_in_progress_seconds` to tickets
- Add `LiveTimer` component with self-contained setInterval for HH:MM:SS display
- Separate Open (status=open only) and In Progress (status=in_progress only) tabs
- Enforce only 1 ticket in progress at a time in handleStartJob and handleSaveChanges
- Add handleMoveBackToOpen that accumulates session seconds when moving back to open
- Add In Progress tab with live green session timers on each ticket card
- Add Move to Open/Move Back to Open buttons on in-progress tickets
- Update ticket details sheet: current session timer, total in-progress time, time since first started
- Update Summary Cards: 5 cards including In Progress count
- Guard against null ticketData in handleStartJob

Co-authored-by: craigbfelt <77277252+craigbfelt@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: AI workflow analysis API + execution panel rewrite

API: new 'analyze' action on /api/workflows
- Sends all step results to GPT-4o-mini with security analyst prompt
- Produces: Executive Verdict, Cross-Step Correlations, Risk Score (0-100),
  Remediation Plan with specific Azure CLI/PowerShell scripts, Next Steps
- Truncates step data to 3K chars each to fit LLM context

Execution Panel rewrite:
- Inline result summaries always visible (e.g., '7 alerts found')
- Collapsible rendered results per step (tables, badges via ResultRenderer)
- Full report builder with untruncated markdown export
- Download .md button alongside Copy Report
- Uses resolvedParams ?? params fallback for custom workflow compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: BLOCkTEL - Android call blocker for French spam prefixes

Block incoming calls from ARCEP-designated telemarketing prefixes:
- 12 metropolitan prefixes (0162, 0163, 0270, 0271, 0377, 0378, 0424, 0425, 0568, 0569, 0948, 0949)
- 5 overseas prefixes (09475-09479)

Uses CallScreeningService (Android 10+), no external dependencies.
Includes prefix management UI, custom prefix support, and blocked call history.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle empty cherry-pick and preserve branch on failure

- Detect already-applied commits during cherry-pick (empty working
  tree + CHERRY_PICK_HEAD present) and auto-skip them with
  git cherry-pick --skip in a loop
- Return success/failure from cherryPickToBase() so teardown can
  decide whether to delete the branch
- On cherry-pick failure, preserve the branch for manual resolution
  instead of silently deleting it

commit.message:
fix: unblock signals — cooldown gate, silent rate-limits, Bot() per message, import overhead (#81)

* Initial plan

* fix: comprehensive signal-blocking gates, logging, and performance improvements

- CRITICAL: Cooldown gate no longer blocks CH3 (Easy Breakout); signal lifecycle
  tracking (TP/SL) runs independently of cooldown; only CH1/CH2 are gated
- CRITICAL: Log WARNING (not DEBUG) when `can_open_signal()` blocks a signal,
  including symbol, side, and max cap count for visibility
- CRITICAL: Log WARNING (not DEBUG) when a signal is rate-limited by
  MIN_SIGNAL_GAP_SECONDS for CH1, CH2, and CH3
- MEDIUM: Move hot-path in-function imports to module level (hard_scalp,
  medium_scalp, easy_breakout, funding_rate, session_filter, correlation_guard)
- MEDIUM: Use shared `_application.bot` in `_broadcast_to_channel` to avoid
  creating a new HTTP session per Telegram message; falls back to fresh Bot
  context when _application is not yet initialised
- MEDIUM: Remove redundant nested async Bot() closures for correlation alerts
  and latency alerts — use _broadcast_to_channel instead
- MEDIUM: Create a single `ThreadPoolExecutor` across all seed batches instead
  of creating/destroying one per batch (both futures and spot seeding)
- LOW: Fix `_check_weight()` ordering in exchange.py — threshold is checked
  BEFORE incrementing so the logged counter is accurate and no over-counting
  occurs when the window boundary is crossed
- LOW: Add `_application` module-level reference, set in `build_application()`
- LOW: Document spot WebSocket `on_candle_close=None` as intentional
- LOW: Reduce `MIN_SIGNAL_GAP_SECONDS` default from 300s (5 min) to 60s in
  both pydantic settings model and env-var fallback in config.py

All 1167 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review feedback — clarify sleep_time ordering and MAX_SAME_SIDE_SIGNALS comment

Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add deduplication and per-conflict Slack notifications

- Created deduplication module with fingerprint-based tracking
  - 42-day expiry for conflict fingerprints
  - Categorizes conflicts as new/changed/unchanged/resolved
  - State stored in .pr-conflict-state.json (gitignored)

- Rewrote slack_notify to send one message per conflict/cluster
  - Uses @mentions (assumes GitHub username = Slack username)
  - Simple 2-PR pairs show details with line ranges
  - Multi-PR clusters show cluster summary
  - Only sends for new + changed conflicts (not unchanged)

- Updated pr_conflict_detector.py to integrate deduplication
  - Loads state, compares conflicts, saves updated state
  - Filters Slack notifications to only new/changed

- Added comprehensive tests
  - test_deduplication.py with 19 tests
  - Updated test_slack_notify.py for new message format
  - Fixed test_pr_conflict_detector.py to mock deduplication

Test/lint status: 177 tests passing, 99% coverage, 9.99/10 pylint

commit.message:
fix(tests): resolve integration test env var collision and anyio cancel scope errors

- test_auth_flow.py: use os.environ.setdefault() instead of direct
  assignment so it does not overwrite ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD
  already set by the test runner (caused 401s in integration tests
  when pytest collected all test modules at startup)
- test_mcp_smoke.py: inline sse_client + ClientSession setup inside
  each test function instead of using a shared async fixture; this
  keeps the anyio cancel scope in the same event loop task as the
  test body, eliminating 'Attempted to exit cancel scope in a
  different task' RuntimeErrors during fixture teardown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use Tailwind hidden class for breadcrumb nav initial state

Replace style="display:none" with the hidden utility class on the
breadcrumb nav, since the JS always sets explicit display values via
inline styles (display:flex / display:none) which override class-based
styles. The error-msg paragraph correctly retains style="display:none"
because its JS handler calls style.display = '' (empty string) to show
it — an inline style removal that would not clear a class-based hidden.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(nvim): add eval watchdog for SSE disconnect detection

Track eval_in_flight state in init.lua. When SSE disconnects during
an active evaluation, fire a 5-second watchdog timer that notifies
the user with an actionable message suggesting :SageFsReconnect.

Cancel the watchdog on reconnect or when eval results arrive normally.

commit.message:
Fix agent prompt: retry on invalid input, resolve target before manifest write

promptAgentChoice now retries up to 3 times on invalid input instead of
failing immediately. This prevents transient empty-input issues from
aborting the install flow.

Move resolveInstallTargets() (which may prompt for agent choice) before
manifest/pinfile writes in both get.go and install.go. Previously, a
failed prompt left dependencies persisted in the manifest, causing
confusing retry behavior where the user's answer was parsed as a URL
argument.

Add 6 tests for promptAgentChoice covering: valid input, Both selection,
retry on empty, retry on garbage, exhausted retries, and EOF.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(client): move changie fragments to correct unreleased directory

Changie multi-project configs expect all fragments in the shared
.changes/unreleased/ directory with a 'project' field inside the YAML
to identify which project they belong to. The fragments were incorrectly
placed in per-project subdirectories (.changes/{project}/unreleased/)
which changie does not search for unreleased changes.

Also adds required 'project' and 'time' fields to the fragments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(vs): capture daemon stderr, add eval watchdog, improve error reporting

DaemonManager: redirect stderr (UseShellExecute=false) so startup
failures include daemon output. Added readStderr() helper.

LiveTestingSubscriber: added ConnectionLost/ConnectionRestored events
that fire when the SSE stream drops or recovers.

EvalService: detects connection loss during in-flight eval and returns
a descriptive error instead of silently failing.

InfoBarService: added ShowDaemonStartupFailedAsync (with stderr) and
ShowEvalInterruptedAsync notifications.

StatusBarManager/DaemonCommands: updated to use Process return type
and show stderr on startup failure.

commit.message:
refactor(SEC-003): mission.py — add exc_info=True to all 20 boundary guards

All 20 except-Exception safety nets in the mission orchestrator now log
full tracebacks via exc_info=True. This makes unexpected failures
diagnosable in production logs without removing the graceful degradation
that prevents orchestrator crashes.

Pattern: specific catches (OSError, ValueError, etc.) handle known
failures; broad catches with exc_info=True catch the unexpected while
producing actionable diagnostics for future narrowing.

30/30 mission tests pass, ruff clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: Bug fixes for running a large/complicated solution on AL-Go (#2075) ### ❔What, Why & How This PR fixes a few minor bugs and 3 major, all changes required for moving Bunker Holding Group to AL-Go for GitHub [Bug] Fix a small spelling mistake in DownloadProjectDependencies.Action.ps1‎ [Bug] Avoid failing entire workflows due to cleanup issues (f.ex. if no Container is used) in PipelineCleanup.ps1 Fixes #2086 - In AL-Go-Helper.ps1 The sort order is changed from this: to this: Which completes in 2:01, saving 45 minutes from the first run, just by re-ordering and allowing people to see compile errors up-front - fail early! The key difference here is just the order in which projects are built/tested. In the first run, build3 had to wait 30 minutes until the test run was done before building the next jobs, and 1 hour and 44 until the last build jobs would run. In the second run - all test jobs are running in parallel at the very end, getting compile errors quicly and postponing test runs until the very last. Subsequently, when using GitHub hosted Custom runners, we can achieve this: Note that the custom runners are running tests in 40 minutes, where the GitHub hosted runners takes 104 minutes. This is because the GitHub hosted custom runners are using SQL Server Developer edition and no docker. Saving a full 2 hours, bringing a complete build from 2 hours 46 minutes down to 46 minutes. Fixes #2085 - In GetDependencies in GitHub-Helper.ps1 When downloading artifacts from projects build earlier in the same workflow, it flags projects as missing if either Apps or TestApps are missing. This means that projects, which only contains TestApps or Apps will be downloaded but also looked for in the last known good build later on. It doesn't fail, because it cannot find the artifacts in that built either. See https://github.com/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21079606240/job/60630194330 Fixes #2084 in GetArtifactsFromWorkflowRun in GitHub-Helper.ps1 If a build is cancelled during tests or failing due to instability - and you subsequently re-run failed jobs (not all jobs) you will get multiple artifacts for succeeding projects and subsequent jobs cannot locate these artifacts (they get an array of artifacts). The "double" artifacts are not visible in the UI, but found when artifacts are queried. You could see this as a bug in GitHub, but we should fix this in AL-Go in a way that works, even if GitHub fixes their bug. This build: https://github.com/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21090442931 Has double artifacts: https://api.github.com/repos/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21090442931/artifacts When than downloading artifacts from prior builds for incremental builds, it fails with ``` throw "Multiple artifacts found with mask $artifactMask for project $projectName" ``` See: https://github.com/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21090506962/job/60661024240 ### ✅ Checklist - [x] Add tests (E2E, unit tests) - [x] Update RELEASENOTES.md - [ ] Update documentation (e.g. for new settings or scenarios) - [ ] Add telemetry --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Maria Zhelezova <43066499+mazhelez@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Alexander Holstrup <117829001+aholstrup1@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: The plug-and-play DevOps solution for Business Central app development on GitHub

Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

commit.message:
chore: スキル棚卸し — ゴースト削除・重複統合・退役 (#152)

* chore: SKILL.md未作成のゴーストディレクトリ4個を削除

削除対象:
- skill-writing-guide (skills-author-skillがカバー)
- skills-generate-skill-template (skills-author-skillがカバー)
- skills-optimize-skill-discoverability (skills-revise-skillがカバー)
- skills-remediate-validation-findings (skills-validate-skill + skills-revise-skillがカバー)

理由: SKILL.mdが存在しないディレクトリはAIエージェントのルーティング時にノイズとなる。
各機能は既存スキルで十分にカバーされている。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: skills-maintain-bilingual-skill を skills-revise-skill に統合

変更内容:
- skills-revise-skill Step 4 に W3.1/W3.2 安全語彙チェックを追加
- 詳細パリティワークフローを references/bilingual-parity.md に退避
- skills-author-skill の Related Skills 参照を更新
- skills-maintain-bilingual-skill ディレクトリを削除

理由: EN/JA同期は revision の部分工程であり、独立スキルとしての分割は過剰。
skills-revise-skill の description にも 'synchronize EN/JA structure' と明記済み。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: skills-refactor-skill-to-single-workflow を退役

変更内容:
- skills-refactor-skill-to-single-workflow を local_reference_skills/ に退避
- 5つの関連スキルから Related Skills 参照を除去
  - skills-author-skill (EN/JA)
  - skills-revise-skill
  - skills-validate-skill
  - skills-generate-skill-suite (Related + Resources)

理由: cosmos modernization（レガシー→シングルワークフロー移行）は完了済み。

commit.message:
fix: make apt-get update non-fatal for third-party repo failures

Third-party repositories (e.g. yarn) with expired GPG keys cause
apt-get update to exit non-zero, which aborts the entire script
under set -euo pipefail. apt-get install still verifies individual
package signatures, so security is not compromised.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Fase 3 — Modul RAB & Anggaran (backend + frontend)

Backend:
- ProyekRepository: CRUD + GetTotalRab/GetTotalRealisasi
- RabRepository: CRUD + LockAll/GetStatusLock
- PengeluaranRepository: CRUD (soft delete) + SumByRabItem
- ProyekService: ListProyek/GetProyek dengan ProyekSummary (alert warning/danger)
- RabService: CRUD + LockRab, ErrRabLocked → HTTP 403, TotalAnggaran = Volume × HargaSatuan
- PengeluaranService: CRUD pengeluaran
- ProyekHandler, RabHandler, PengeluaranHandler: 14 route baru
- Router: RBAC — owner+manajer CRUD proyek/RAB; owner-only delete & lock RAB

Frontend:
- AppLayout + Sidebar: navigasi responsif, mobile hamburger, user info, logout
- ProyekCard: status badge, progress bar (alert_level aware), info ringkas
- Halaman Proyek: grid + filter 5 status + skeleton + empty state + modal tambah
- ProyekDetail: tabs RAB / Pengeluaran / Info + modal edit
- RabTab: donut chart, summary cards, alert banner, tabel, lock RAB + confirm dialog
- PengeluaranTab: tabel pengeluaran, total, modal form
- useProyek: semua query + mutation hooks (TanStack Query v5)
- App.tsx: route /proyek dan /proyek/:id dengan AppLayout + ProtectedRoute

Verifikasi:
  go build ./...  → 0 error
  tsc --noEmit    → 0 error TypeScript

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Year 7 Maths practice assessment webpage

Indices & Computations with Positive and Negative Integers practice exam

commit.message:
refactor: 使用 Asset Tag 作为资产编号 (asset_id)

- asset_manager.py: 移除 asset_tag 字段，asset_id 直接使用 SAP Asset Tag
  - create_asset() 必须由用户提供 asset_id，不再自动生成 AST-xxxx
  - SAP导入时用 Asset Tag 列值作为 asset_id
  - 无有效 Asset Tag 的行跳过（不再用 N/A 占位）
  - 新增重复 asset_id 检查
- agent_engine.py: 更新 prompt 说明资产编号由用户提供
  - 新建资产时要求提供编号，不能自编
  - 引导 Agent 先搜索再操作

DataScaffold.GetEnumLookup() dictionary for wire-format enum parsing.

and DateTime handling before Convert.ChangeType fallback.

AdminAssistantService: add inline enum name→value lookup before
Convert.ChangeType fallback (AI tool path, not hot).

Closes #1373
Closes #1374

commit.message:
fix(release): sync manifest versions and auto-trigger build (#858)

* fix(release): sync manifest versions and auto-trigger build on release

Source manifest files (gemini-extension.json, plugin.json, marketplace.json)
were never updated by release-please, causing Gemini CLI to install stale
versions. Added them as extra-files in release-please config. Also added
workflow_dispatch step to trigger build-extension after release, since
GITHUB_TOKEN cannot trigger other workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: register run_id in _active_mission_tasks (watchdog checks run_id not session_id)

The watchdog's duplicate detection checks run_id (mission/epic ID) in
_active_mission_tasks, but previous fix only registered session_id.
Now register BOTH keys so watchdog correctly sees the builder is running
and doesn't re-launch it after 5 minutes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
